original_image_tags: A line graph with a logarithmic Y-axis labeled "Percent in 5-deg sector (%)" ranging from 0.01 to 100. The X-axis is labeled "Angle From Flight Path (deg)" ranging from 0 to 180. The graph title is "LLV1 Random-Attitude Failures through 290 sec". A legend box titled "Breakup q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²" lists symbols for "no breakup", "20,000", "10,000", and "5,000". Four fitted curves are plotted and labeled: "A = 2.45, B = 10,000", "A = 2.60, B = 1,000", "A = 2.70, B = 1,000", and "A = 2.75, B = 1,000". Data points are plotted along these curves.

elements.description: A line chart titled 'LLV1 Random-Attitude Failures through 290 sec'. The y-axis is labeled 'Percent in 5-deg sector (%)' on a logarithmic scale from 0.01 to 100. The x-axis is labeled 'Angle From Flight Path (deg)' from 0 to 180. A legend lists data points for 'Breakup q-alpha in deg-lb/ft^2' including 'no breakup' (triangle), '20,000' (filled circle), '10,000' (open circle), and '5,000' (square). Four plotted lines represent different parameter combinations: 'A = 2.45, B = 10,000' (solid line), 'A = 2.60, B = 1,000' (dashed line), 'A = 2.70, B = 1,000' (dash-dot line), and 'A = 2.75, B = 1,000' (solid line). The caption below reads 'Figure 31. LLV1 Simulation Results with Best-Fit Shaping Constants'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the chart title, axis labels, scale, legend categories, plotted line parameters, and overall visual structure without contradiction.
